commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=df4e97f18ca9e16af6f1407d0cc4699636e348a5)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds optional `filter`, `sort`, `field_names`, and `view_id` arguments to `feishu_bitable_list_records`, using `appTableRecord.search` when filtering or sorting is requested and the existing list endpoint otherwise.

PR surface: Source +60. Total +60 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: the PR diff sends a string `filter` to `appTableRecord.search`, while the pinned Lark SDK 1.66.0 types require a structured filter object. I did not run a live Feishu table because this is a read-only review and no contributor proof was provided.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the canonical Feishu Bitable endpoint/tool contract and the external contributor needs real behavior proof before repair or merge automation is safe.

target.title: feat(feishu): add filter, sort, field_names, view_id to feishu_bitable_list_records
target.body:
## Summary

Adds server-side filtering, sorting, field selection, and view scoping to `feishu_bitable_list_records`.

## Changes

- **`filter`**: Feishu filter expression syntax (e.g. `AND(CurrentValue.[Status]="Open")`)
- **`sort`**: Array of `"field_name:desc"` strings, parsed into Lark SDK format
- **`field_names`**: Only return specified fields (reduces payload)
- **`view_id`**: Scope query to a specific view

When `filter` or `sort` is provided, switches from the GET `.list()` to the POST `.search()` endpoint which supports server-side filtering/sorting. Falls back to `.list()` for simple queries.

All parameters are optional — fully backward-compatible, no breaking changes.

## Motivation

The bitable tool is significantly less useful without filtering/sorting since agents must fetch all records and filter client-side, which is slow and token-wasteful for large tables.

## Previous PR

This is a resubmission of #36347 which was auto-closed for inactivity.
target.author: manusjs
